creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Paul Floyd from comment #14)
> How have things changed between 13.2 and 14.0 to cause this?

I don't see anything kernel related that's different between 13.2 and 14.0. Is it possible that we had a larger .data section when building for 13.2? If the PT_LOAD is larger than a page I think we'd have a rw- mapping of type vn.

If I understand correctly Valgrind doesn't actually check the contents of the mappings, it just wants to check that they exist? If that's the case I think it'd be reasonable to just relax the check.
